app.name: GPT Image 2 (ChatGPT)
app.bestFor: Tops the Artificial Analysis and LMArena image arenas for prompt adherence and text. Best all-round pick.
app.rankBasis: Ranks first or near first across all three cited 2026 comparisons, supported by leading arena results for instruction following, editing and legible text.
app.pricingFree: ChatGPT Free includes limited image generation with rate limits
app.pricingPaid: ChatGPT Plus $20/mo — expanded image generation, editing and access to higher-capability models
pros: excellent prompt adherence and iterative editing
strong text rendering and layout understanding
integrated naturally into a general assistant workflow
cons: strict rate limits on the free plan
less low-level control than node-based local tools
safety filters can reject benign edge cases

app.name: Nano Banana Pro (Google Gemini)
app.bestFor: Leads on photorealism, character consistency and UGC ad creatives. Tops CNET's 2026 ranking.
app.rankBasis: Placed at or near the top of all three cited comparisons for photorealism and character consistency, with particularly strong reviewer support for product and UGC-style creative.
app.pricingFree: Gemini Free includes limited image generation and editing subject to daily capacity
app.pricingPaid: Google AI Pro $19.99/mo — higher Gemini limits plus 1,000 monthly AI credits for supported creative tools
pros: strong photorealism and character consistency
excellent for iterative edits using natural language
good typography and commercial-creative output
cons: limits and available model variants change by region
creative styling can be less distinctive than Midjourney
advanced usage consumes shared Google AI credits

app.name: Midjourney
app.bestFor: Still the king of artistic style — concept art, moodboards, stylized campaigns (V8.1).
app.rankBasis: Appears in all three cited image-generator roundups and remains their most consistent recommendation for distinctive artistic style and concept work.
app.pricingPaid: Basic $10/mo — 3.3 hours of Fast GPU time monthly with unlimited relaxed image generations unavailable on this entry tier
pros: exceptionally strong aesthetics with minimal prompting
large community and mature style vocabulary
fast ideation for concept art and campaigns
cons: no standing free plan
less precise for iterative local edits than conversational tools
subscription uses GPU-time accounting that takes learning

app.name: FLUX.2 (Black Forest Labs)
app.bestFor: Strongest open-weight option. Go-to for product photography and embedding generation into an app.
app.rankBasis: Included in all three cited 2026 comparisons as a leading open-weight/API family, especially for product imagery and developer-controlled deployment.
app.pricingFree: Eligible FLUX open-weight models can be self-hosted under model-specific licenses; compute is not included
app.pricingPaid: BFL API is consumption-based per generated image; rates vary by FLUX model and output settings
pros: excellent image quality with deployment flexibility
strong product photography and prompt adherence
large third-party tooling and hosting ecosystem
cons: licenses differ across models and may restrict commercial use
self-hosting needs substantial GPU resources
API price varies by model and resolution

app.name: Ideogram
app.bestFor: Best at text inside images — logos, posters, typography. Generous free tier.
app.rankBasis: Both cited roundups distinguish Ideogram for typography and graphic-design prompts, giving it a clear specialist reputation despite less overall adoption than the top four.
app.pricingFree: Free plan with a weekly allowance of slow-generation credits and public creations
app.pricingPaid: Basic from $8/mo — monthly priority credits, private generation and expanded editing/download features
pros: best-in-class legible text for posters and logos
easy browser workflow
useful design-oriented editing tools
cons: free creations are public and use slow credits
less flexible ecosystem than open models
photorealism and complex compositions can be inconsistent

app.name: Stable Diffusion 3.5
app.bestFor: Self-hosted, fully controllable generation with a huge ecosystem of fine-tunes and LoRAs.
app.rankBasis: The two cited comparisons continue to select Stable Diffusion for self-hosting and control, while its broader ecosystem of fine-tunes, LoRAs and interfaces remains unmatched.
app.pricingFree: Free self-hosting under the Stability Community License for eligible users; hardware and hosting costs are separate
app.pricingPaid: Stability API uses prepaid credits; published credit packs start at $10 for 1,000 credits and model usage varies
features: Open model weights for local generation
pros: maximum control and privacy when self-hosted
largest customization ecosystem
works across many local and cloud interfaces
cons: setup and model selection are complex
high-quality local use requires capable hardware
license eligibility and API credit math require attention

app.name: DALL-E
app.bestFor: Easiest on-ramp for beginners, bundled inside ChatGPT. Increasingly superseded by GPT Image 2.
app.rankBasis: Only one cited 2026 source still recommends DALL-E, and OpenAI's newer GPT Image line has superseded it for ChatGPT image generation, so its current relevance is primarily legacy API compatibility.
app.pricingPaid: Legacy DALL-E API usage is charged per image; current ChatGPT subscriptions use GPT Image rather than a separate DALL-E plan
pros: historically simple and widely documented API
good compatibility with existing integrations
requires little image-generation expertise
cons: superseded by GPT Image for new OpenAI workflows
no separate consumer product or free plan
weaker editing and text rendering than current models

skill.name: Stable Diffusion WebUI (AUTOMATIC1111)
skill.bestFor: The most-adopted local image-generation tool ever (~164k GitHub★) — the de-facto Stable Diffusion interface with a vast extension ecosystem.
skill.rankBasis: Approximately 164k GitHub stars and top billing in the cited WebUI comparison make AUTOMATIC1111 the most adopted local Stable Diffusion interface by a wide margin.
pros: largest user community and tutorial ecosystem
broad model and extension compatibility
powerful controls in a familiar web interface
cons: interface is dense and dated
extension conflicts and updates can break setups
performance and installation depend heavily on local hardware

skill.name: ComfyUI
skill.bestFor: The node-based industry standard (~122k GitHub★) for pro Stable Diffusion / FLUX pipelines; 12k+ custom nodes and full pipeline control.
skill.rankBasis: About 122k GitHub stars, 12k+ community nodes and strong placement in the cited comparison establish ComfyUI as the professional node-workflow standard.
features: Broad SD, FLUX and video-model support
pros: complete control over every generation stage
efficiently reuses models and intermediate results
fast-moving ecosystem supports new models early
cons: steep learning curve for node graphs
custom-node dependency conflicts are common
complex workflows are difficult to debug and maintain

skill.name: Fooocus (lllyasviel)
skill.bestFor: The simplest local generator (~51k GitHub★) — a Midjourney-like, prompt-only experience on SDXL. Best on-ramp for non-technical users.
skill.rankBasis: Roughly 51k GitHub stars and inclusion in the cited open-source UI roundup make Fooocus the best-adopted simplicity-first local generator after the two major power-user interfaces.
pros: easiest local interface for beginners
good defaults reduce prompt and sampler tuning
produces polished SDXL results quickly
cons: project is in limited maintenance mode
far less workflow control than ComfyUI
new model support lags more active interfaces

skill.name: Diffusers (Hugging Face)
skill.bestFor: The standard library (~34k GitHub★) for running/embedding diffusion models (SD, SDXL, FLUX) into your own app — the developer default.
skill.rankBasis: Approximately 34k GitHub stars and first-party Hugging Face integration across major model releases make Diffusers the default developer library rather than an end-user UI.
pros: standardized API across many model families
strong documentation and active maintenance
best fit for embedding generation into applications
cons: requires Python and ML engineering knowledge
not a ready-made creative interface
dependency and GPU compatibility can be difficult

skill.name: InvokeAI
skill.bestFor: Clean, professional local studio (~27.7k GitHub★) with a powerful unified canvas — combines SD WebUI and ComfyUI strengths for creative workflows.
skill.rankBasis: About 27.7k GitHub stars support substantial adoption, with its Unified Canvas giving InvokeAI a distinct professional-studio position among local interfaces.
skill.pricingPaid: Commercial hosted and team offerings are available separately; public pricing may require sales contact
pros: polished interface for iterative creative work
canvas bridges simple editing and advanced workflows
good model and gallery management
cons: heavier installation than simpler local UIs
smaller extension ecosystem than AUTOMATIC1111 or ComfyUI
some team features belong to commercial offerings

skill.name: Stability-AI generative-models (SDXL / SD3)
skill.bestFor: Stability AI's official model repo (~27.2k GitHub★) — reference SDXL / SD3 weights and code from the maker of Stable Diffusion.
skill.rankBasis: Approximately 27.2k GitHub stars and official-source status make this the reference implementation for SDXL/SD3 research, though it is less convenient than application-focused interfaces.
skill.pricingFree: Free source code and eligible model use under Stability's model-specific licenses; self-hosting costs apply
pros: authoritative code from the model publisher
valuable for research and custom training
transparent architecture and configuration
cons: research repository rather than an end-user app
model licenses are not uniformly unrestricted open source
setup and hardware demands are substantial

skill.name: FLUX (Black Forest Labs)
skill.bestFor: Official inference repo for FLUX (~25.8k GitHub★) — the strongest open-weight image model family, go-to for product photography quality.
skill.rankBasis: About 25.8k GitHub stars and official inference code give FLUX the strongest direct adoption signal among newer open-weight image-model repositories.
skill.pricingFree: Free source code; model weights are governed by model-specific licenses and self-hosting costs apply
pros: high-quality official implementation
strong photorealism and prompt adherence
widely integrated into community tooling
cons: large models require substantial VRAM
some weights restrict commercial use
repository is code-first with no polished creator UI

skill.name: nano-banana-pro (intellectronica/agent-skills)
skill.bestFor: The most-cited image Agent Skill — generate/edit images up to 4K with Nano Banana Pro (Gemini 3 Pro Image), strong on legible in-image text.
skill.rankBasis: The skill is repeatedly indexed by the cited skill directories and benefits from adoption of the broader intellectronica/agent-skills collection, but lacks a clean standalone usage count.
skill.pricingFree: Free & open-source skill; limited Gemini API free quota may apply
skill.pricingPaid: Gemini API usage is consumption-based when free quota is exhausted
features: Gemini API setup and file handling
pros: simple agent workflow for generation and editing
strong in-image text and instruction following
portable skill with inspectable instructions
cons: requires a Gemini API key and external billing
inherits model rate limits and regional availability
adoption signal is mostly from the parent collection

skill.name: ccskill-nanobanana (feedtailor)
skill.bestFor: Claude Code image-generation skill on the Gemini 3 Pro Image (Nano Banana Pro) model. Clean, well-documented but single-author.
skill.rankBasis: A documented public skill with one cited independent walkthrough, but it remains a low-adoption single-author wrapper with limited community evidence.
skill.pricingFree: Free & open-source skill; limited Gemini API free quota may apply
skill.pricingPaid: Gemini API usage is consumption-based when free quota is exhausted
pros: small and easy to inspect
straightforward Claude Code setup
supports both generation and edits
cons: very limited maintenance and adoption signal
depends entirely on Gemini API access
narrower workflow and documentation than the higher-ranked skill

notes: Rebuilt to rank by real reputation/adoption, not form factor (kind is a metadata tag only). The open-source image-generation tools dominate and correctly outrank the agent skills: AUTOMATIC1111's WebUI (~164k★) and ComfyUI (~122k★) are the de-facto interfaces, followed by Fooocus, Diffusers, InvokeAI, Stability's official models and FLUX. The Nano-Banana Gemini-wrapper skills are genuine but far lower adoption, so they sit at the bottom. Previous MCP-server entries were removed.
